title: Carvana’s bet on Slate is ACTUALLY a bet on itself, as the future of automotive retail
summary:
As new electric vehicle brands like Scout and Slate search for alternatives to franchise auto dealers, America’s largest online auto retailer is quietly positioning itself to become the future of automotive retail.

title: Wallbox deploys its first Supernova PowerRing DC fast chargers
summary:
Wallbox just installed its first public Supernova PowerRing DC fast chargers at Port de Sitges, a busy marina on Spain’s Mediterranean coast near Barcelona.

title: T1 Energy buys KORE Power to cash in on the AI power boom
summary:
T1 Energy, formerly known as FREYR Battery, is buying battery storage and software company KORE Power in a deal aimed at growing its presence in the fast-expanding energy storage and AI data center markets.

title: Volkswagen’s push for more affordable EVs starts with these two
summary:
The first Volkswagen ID. Polo and Cupra Raval models rolled off the production line at the Group’s Martorell plant in Spain, the first of several new affordable, mass-market EVs.

more…

title: BYD’s ‘world’s first’ 1,000-km range luxury GT is off to a hot start
summary:
The new Denza Z9 GT can drive over 1,000 km (621 miles) without stopping, fully recharge in under 10 minutes, and starts at about $40,000 in China. Now, BYD is unleashing it in Europe, the UK, and several other markets.

more…

title: Tesla pushes Roadster demo to August as SpaceX thruster work continues
summary:
Tesla has pushed the planned public demo of its next-generation Roadster to August or later, according to a report from The Information citing four people with knowledge of the program. It marks yet another delay for the vehicle first unveiled as a prototype nearly nine years ago.

The demo, expected to take place in Texas, is meant to showcase the SpaceX cold gas thruster system that Tesla claims will allow the Roadster to achieve extreme acceleration and even lift off the ground.

more…

summary:
In the Electrek Podcast, we discuss the most popular news in the world of sustainable transport and energy. In this week’s episode, we discuss Tesla Semi nailing a big test, BMW iX3’s range impressing in a new test, Xpeng catching up to Tesla, and more.

summary:
We’ve got some notable headliners closing out this week’s Green Deals, starting with Yozma’s Anniversary Sale that is offering up to $1,043+ in savings when using our exclusive code, giving you $500 price cuts and $448 FREE bundles on the IN 10 Electric Mini Dirt Bike and IN 10 Pro Electric Dirt Bike starting from $1,044, not to mention further savings playing its prize wheel game. We also have Lectric’s newly launched Father’s Day Sale that is offering up to $893 bundle savings on e-bikes starting from $799. From there, you can find the new Segway Navimow i215 LiDAR Robot Lawn Mower with second-ever savings to its $1,399 low, among other models – plus, EGO’s 10-inch and 18-inch misting fans, Greenworks’ 60V 20-inch pole hedge trimmer, and GOOLOO’s 12-in-1 A7 jump starter + air compressor. And don’t forget about the hangover deals collected down at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s exclusive 3,584Wh Jackery HomePower 3600 Plus power station + bundle deals, the new low on EGO’s Select Cut Touch Drive mower bundle, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Range Rover is finally ready to sell its first electric SUV: Here’s everything we know
summary:
Range Rover has confirmed the electric SUV is still set to go on sale in the next few months, after it was delayed last year.

summary:
Toyota’s new electric SUVs are selling surprisingly well, becoming top-sellers in several markets. So, why is it pulling back now?

title: Slate Could Have A Powerful New Friend In Carvana
summary: One online car dealer may have quietly secured its place in making Slate's dreams come true, according to a report.

title: Volkswagen Compares Gas Cars To Horses, Says That EVs Are Plain Better
summary: Horses were never banned, but people realized it’s easier to use gas cars to travel. VW says the same will happen with EVs.

title: Audi's Stunning New Nuvolari Supercar Is A Tri-Motor Plug-In Hybrid
summary: Audi’s most powerful car ever pairs three tiny axial flux motors with a screaming V-8 engine.

title: Tesla Wants Its Cameras To Blink Like a Human Eye
summary: Computers might be better at some things, but Tesla's newest patent shows that the human eye remains undefeated in one important area.

title: This 85% Scale Vintage Bentley Is One Of A Kind—And Electric
summary: The bespoke Bentley Blower Junior was commissioned by American actor Jason Momoa.

title: The Polestar 2 Is Returning To North America
summary: The fan favorite EV is going back on sale in North America, thanks to Canada’s openness to Chinese-built electric cars.

title: The Volvo XC60 Plug-In Hybrid Will Get A Serious Range Boost: Report
summary: The current XC60 T8 plug-in hybrid can go 35 miles on electric power. The 2028 model will reportedly nearly triple that.

title: It's Still Way More Expensive To Insure An EV. But That's Changing
summary: Newer EVs cost about 18% more to insure than gas vehicles. Here's why.

title: The Rolls-Royce Spectre Gets Big Update. But You Wouldn’t Know It
summary: It now goes farther, charges faster, and responds more sharply, but the design remains mostly untouched.

title: The Company That Builds The iPhone Just Unveiled A Tesla Model Y Competitor
summary: It’s no Apple Car, but Foxconn’s Cavira gets an LFP battery and a dual-motor powertrain that can deliver up to 468 horsepower.
